objective: Create the Handbook Contract Membrane control pack, orchestration onboarding prompt, durable handoff ledger, escalation protocol, and active documentation pointers without changing Rust.
work_completed: Created the active target-architecture, semantic-model, seam-crosswalk, phase/slice, contract/schema, and proof-ledger documents.
Created a ready-to-use orchestration onboarding prompt and finding-driven decomposition rules.
Created immutable per-session handoff records, a rebuildable JSONL query index, JSON Schemas, a record template, and a durable dispatch template.
Updated the active documentation front door and idea-document authority notes.
Recorded the greenfield rule prohibiting user migration tooling and permanent legacy dual authority.

deliverables.summary: Control-pack router, authority, selective context, landing labels, greenfield rule, and session closeout contract.
Target owner, SDK, CLI, skill, Tauri, Substrate, projection, synthesis, and dock architecture.
Profile, artifact, vocabulary, Context Resolution, projection, memory, promotion, and adapter semantics.
Live current-to-target seam classifications and coupling zones.
Provisional Phase 0 through Phase 6 decomposition and finding-driven child-packet protocol.
Preliminary typed profile, Resolution, projection, SDK/JSON, contract, evidence, gate, and dock contracts.
Current proof tiers, open gates, preserved baselines, and temporary-bridge deletion policy.
Ready-to-use latest/specific-handoff orchestration prompt and dispatch contract.
Session status, escalation, immutable record, JSONL index, jq query, dispatch, and short-chat protocols.

decisions.summary: Use a durable repository-owned control pack with selective per-slice context assembly.
decisions.authority_effect: The control pack becomes program architecture and sequencing authority after review.

decisions.summary: Use immutable per-session JSON handoff records plus a rebuildable JSONL index rather than one mutable master record.
decisions.authority_effect: Session closeout and orchestration selection are durable and queryable without chat report copying.

decisions.summary: Treat the architecture as greenfield and prohibit user migration tooling or permanent legacy dual authority.
decisions.authority_effect: Implementation slices define target truth directly; temporary internal bridges require deletion gates.

decisions.summary: Keep the installed Handbook skill as the agent-facing onboarding/orchestration adapter over deterministic CLI/SDK operations.
decisions.authority_effect: Core projection remains deterministic; any future Handbook synthesis must use Unified Agent API programmatically and remain candidate-only.

findings.summary: The newly created control pack has not yet received the required independent cohesion/contradiction/repo-truth review, so HCM-0.1 cannot close as frozen implementation authority.

validation.command: jq empty on handoff JSON artifacts and jq -s on ledger.jsonl
Python jsonschema Draft 2020-12 validation of schemas and handoff template
Repository-relative Markdown link validation for active docs and control pack

pack_updates.reason: Point concise architecture lineage to the active pack and record newly approved semantic commitments.
Make the control pack the active front door.
Prevent the older Substrate-owned draft from overriding current Handbook ownership.

next_session.objective: Select this handoff, revalidate the control-pack bootstrap, and dispatch a fresh docs-only independent review of HCM-0.1 before any implementation packet is created.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh review checks contradiction, cohesion, concreteness, repo-truth accuracy, link/schema validity, and orchestration usability.
Actionable findings are remediated through a new bounded dispatch and durable handoff.
HCM-0.1 closes only after the control-pack proof gate is satisfied.
stop_conditions: Stop and record an escalation if review exposes a target-architecture decision not already approved.
Do not create the first Rust implementation packet before HCM-0.1 review closes.

chat_summary: Bootstrapped the full Handbook Contract Membrane control pack, orchestration onboarding prompt, immutable handoff ledger, escalation protocol, and active docs pointers. HCM-0.1 is ready for orchestration-led independent docs review; no Rust changed.
